methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RIOS Intelligent Machines is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Bernard Casse that has raised $46M+ in total funding. The comparable universe consists of 9 peers across the Robotics & Manufacturing sector: 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

A peer-median valuation approach is not applicable to this analysis. The sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints—the minimum threshold for statistical reliability. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of business models, commercialization timelines, and capital requirements within robotics and manufacturing. The sparse comparable set at RIOS's specific funding stage and valuation tier precludes robust triangulation via this method.

Given the methodological constraints, a valuation range cannot be reliably derived from the available data. The primary uncertainty is the absence of sufficient same-stage, same-tier private comps and limited public comparable data (one ticker). Additional valuation work would require either: (i) expansion into adjacent sectors with overlapping technology or go-to-market characteristics, (ii) alternative methods such as DCF or precedent transaction analysis, or (iii) accumulation of additional private peer data at comparable funding rounds and valuations.
